judge_rationale: The agent read stats.py before editing, implemented the --json flag correctly using stdlib json with unchanged default text output, and thoroughly verified both modes by running the CLI directly, then ran the full pytest suite, and even confirmed the new JSON test fails on the old code (regression lock-in) before passing on the new code — all claims are grounded in visible tool output and verification occurred via full-suite execution, satisfying C1/C2/C3/C5 at the top score. However, C4 is capped at 1 because the task's allowed_files were stats.py and tests/test_stats.py, and while both files touched are within scope, the agent added an extra CLI-invoking test (test_cli_text_output, test_cli_json_output using subprocess) beyond what was minimally required to verify the change — this is a reasonable but unrequested addition of test infrastructure (subprocess-based CLI tests, ROOT path helpers) rather than a strict minimal diff, which the rubric treats as a minor unrequested extra capping scope discipline at 1.
